title: Dynabeads mRNA Purification
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bhegq","text":"The following protocol describes mRNA isolation using 75 μg of total RNA as starting material. This protocol can be scaled up or down by increasing or decreasing reagent volumes proportionally with any changes in the amount of the total RNA starting material. Optimization may be needed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"ba96","text":"Work RNAse-free and wear gloves. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3sk07","text":"Keep the Dynabeads‱ Oligo (dT)25 in liquid suspension during storage and handling steps. Resuspend well before use. warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"e0qkp","text":"Dynabeads‱ Oligo (dT)25 contains 5 mg/mL of beads in phosphate buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.4, with 0.05% Tween‱ and 0.02% sodium azide as a preservative.  Caution: Sodium azide may react with lead and copper plumbing to form highly explosive metal azides. See \"Description of materials\" for Buffer content.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":9,"length":1},{"key":1,"offset":110,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"A03344B096F811EF85840A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}},"1":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"A033453196F811EF85840A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"pavn","text":"Kit contents:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2i25s","text":"Dynabeads‱ Oligo (dT)25 ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":9,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"6vqme","text":"Binding Buffer ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bd9vd","text":"Washing Buffer B ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dfusd","text":"10 mM Tris-HCl","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71gfq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d3a1f","text":"User provided materials:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9fp6k","text":"• DynaMag‱ Magnet  (See thermofisher.com/magnets for recommendations)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":9,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"cokct","text":"• Sample mixer allowing tilting and rotation of tubes (e.g. HulaMixer‱  Sample Mixer) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":69,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2klmi","text":"• Sterile RNase-free microcentrifuge tubes ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ftj8m","text":"• Sterile RNase-free pipette tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"9168411F96F811EF85840A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}},"1":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"7FA8B45D96F811EFBCBF0A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}},"2":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"7FA8B49996F811EFBCBF0A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
steps: Adjust the volume of the total RNA sample (75 μg) to 100 μL with distilled DEPC-treated water, or with 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5. Omit this step if only a small adjustment is needed (see step 6 of “Prepare Dynabeads‱ magnetic beads”).
Heat the sample to 65°C for 2 minutes to disrupt secondary structures.

Transfer 200 μL (1 mg) of resuspended Dynabeads‱ magnetic beads to a microcentrifuge tube. Place the tube on the magnet for 30 seconds, or until all the Dynabeads‱ magnetic beads have adhered to the tube wall.

Discard the supernatant, remove the tube from the magnet, and add 100 μL of Binding Buffer to equilibriate the beads. Place the tube on the magnet and remove the supernatant. Remove the tube from the magnet.

Add 100 μL Binding Buffer to the Dynabeads‱ magnetic beads. Optimal hybridization conditions require a 1:1 ratio of Binding Buffer to sample volume. If the total RNA is more dilute than 75 μg/100 μL, then add a volume of Binding Buffer equal to the sample volume to the Dynabeads‱ magnetic beads.

Add the total RNA to the Dynabeads‱/Binding Buffer suspension. Mix thoroughly, and rotate on a roller or mixer for 3–5 minutes at room temperature to allow the mRNA to anneal to the oligo (dT)25 on the beads.
Place the tube on the magnet until the solution is clear. Remove the supernatant.

Remove the tube from the magnet and wash the mRNA-bead complex twice with 200 μL Washing Buffer B. Use the magnet to remove all traces of supernatant between each washing step (this is important when working with small volumes).
(Optional) Add 10–20 μL (or down to 5 μL) of 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5 to elute the mRNA.
Heat the sample at 65°C to 80°C for 2 minutes and place the tube immediately on the magnet.
Transfer the eluted mRNA to a new RNase-free tube.
creator: Elisabeth Rebboah
